title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
During an investigation, Colonel Weston and Hercule Poirot question Gracie regarding Mrs. Marshall. Gracie describes Mrs. Marshall as an actress-like woman who is often rude and unpredictable. She reveals rumors of an affair between Mrs. Marshall and Mr. Redfin, noting that Mrs. Marshall seems to fear her husband, Mr. Marshall, despite his quiet nature. Regarding the morning's events, Gracie explains she delivered approximately six or seven letters to Mrs. Marshall's breakfast tray, which the woman later tore up and threw in the trash.

Poirot inquires about the cleaning of Linda Marshall's room, which Gracie cleaned around 9:15 AM after she went to breakfast. Gracie notes that Linda Marshall returned to her room at 9:45 AM and left again at 10:30 AM. Poirot also establishes that while Mr. Marshall and Mr. Redfin went swimming before breakfast, Mrs. Marshall's swimsuit remained dry.

When asked if any bottles were missing from the rooms, Gracie admits she could not tell if anything was gone from Mrs. Marshall's room due to the large number of items kept there. Poirot explains his reasoning to Weston: a bottle was recently dropped from an east-side window, nearly hitting Miss Bustard. Poirot deduces that someone threw the bottle into the sea to hide its identity rather than using a wastebasket. Finally, Gladys Nairne reports a "strange" occurrence: the sound of someone taking a bath at midday, which she noted to a colleague.
